title: ascium adamantium
side_effects: Ascium adamantium can have side effects if used improperly, such as severe allergic reactions, respiratory failure, and cardiovascular collapse. Ingesting the plant's toxic sap can lead to nausea, vomiting, and stomach pain, while prolonged exposure to its volatile oils can cause headaches, dizziness, and memory loss. Prolonged inhalation of the plant's spores can also trigger asthma attacks and exacerbate respiratory conditions like COPD. In extreme cases, improper use of Ascium adamantium can lead to coma and even death.
